values.title: Welcome to City of Maplewood
values.hero_description: The City of Maplewood is committed to providing high-quality services, maintaining safe neighborhoods, and fostering a vibrant community for all residents.
values.cta_description: Have a question, concern, or need to report an issue? We are here to serve you. Reach out to our team for assistance.

values.title: Public Works
values.body:
The Department of Public Works maintains and improves the city's infrastructure, including roads, bridges, water systems, and stormwater management. Our team of engineers and field crews work year-round to keep Maplewood safe and functional.

Key Responsibilities

  • Road maintenance and repair
  • Water and sewer systems
  • Stormwater management
  • Snow removal and street cleaning
  • Capital improvement projects

values.title: Parks & Recreation
values.body:
The Parks & Recreation Department manages over 42 parks, community centers, athletic facilities, and recreational programs throughout Maplewood. We are dedicated to enhancing quality of life through accessible outdoor spaces and engaging programs for all ages.

Facilities

  • Community centers and pools
  • Sports fields and courts
  • Nature trails and greenways
  • Playgrounds and splash pads
  • Senior activity centers

values.title: Public Safety
values.body:
The Department of Public Safety oversees police, fire, and emergency services for the City of Maplewood. Our mission is to protect lives, property, and the rights of all people through professional law enforcement and emergency response.

Divisions

  • Police Department
  • Fire Department
  • Emergency Management
  • Code Enforcement
  • Animal Control

values.title: Community Development
values.body:
The Community Development Department guides the physical growth and revitalization of Maplewood through planning, zoning, building inspection, and economic development initiatives. We work to create a sustainable, livable community.

Services

  • Building permits and inspections
  • Zoning and land use planning
  • Historic preservation
  • Economic development
  • Housing programs

values.title: Mayor Patricia Chen
values.body: Mayor Patricia Chen has served the City of Maplewood since 2020. She brings over 15 years of public service experience and is dedicated to transparent governance, sustainable development, and community engagement. Under her leadership, the city has invested heavily in infrastructure improvements and green initiatives.

values.title: David Rodriguez
values.body: David Rodriguez serves as City Manager, overseeing the daily operations of all city departments. With a background in public administration and urban planning, he brings a strategic approach to municipal governance and a commitment to operational excellence.

values.title: Sarah Thompson
values.body: Council Member Sarah Thompson represents District 1 on the Maplewood City Council. She is a longtime resident and advocate for neighborhood revitalization, affordable housing, and improved public transit. She chairs the Finance Committee and serves on the Public Works Committee.

values.title: James Mitchell
values.body: Fire Chief James Mitchell has led the Maplewood Fire Department for over 8 years. A 25-year veteran of firefighting, Chief Mitchell has modernized the department's equipment and training programs while maintaining the highest standards of emergency response.

values.title: Building Permits
values.body:
Apply for building permits for new construction, renovations, additions, and demolition projects within the City of Maplewood. Our building inspectors ensure all projects comply with local building codes and safety standards.

Permit Types

  • New construction permits
  • Renovation and alteration permits
  • Electrical, plumbing, and mechanical permits
  • Demolition permits
  • Sign permits

Processing Time

Standard permits are processed within 5-10 business days. Expedited review is available for an additional fee.

values.title: Water & Sewer Services
values.body:
The City of Maplewood provides safe, clean drinking water and reliable sewer services to all residents and businesses. Our water treatment plant processes over 10 million gallons daily, meeting or exceeding all EPA standards.

Services Available

  • Start or stop water service
  • Pay your utility bill online
  • Report a water main break
  • Request a water quality test
  • Apply for sewer connection
values.fee: Based on usage - see rate schedule

values.title: Park & Facility Rentals
values.body:
Reserve city parks, pavilions, community centers, and athletic facilities for your next event. Whether you are planning a birthday party, corporate retreat, or community gathering, we have spaces to fit your needs.

Available Facilities

  • Park pavilions and picnic areas
  • Community center banquet halls
  • Athletic fields and courts
  • Swimming pool party rentals
  • Amphitheater and outdoor stages
values.eligibility: Open to all - resident discounts available

values.title: Business License Application
values.body:
All businesses operating within Maplewood city limits are required to obtain a business license. Our streamlined application process makes it easy to get started or renew your existing license.

License Types

  • General business license
  • Home occupation permit
  • Food service license
  • Alcohol beverage license
  • Special event vendor permit

values.title: City Council Regular Meeting - March 2026
values.body:
Regular meeting of the Maplewood City Council. The agenda includes the FY2027 budget discussion, updates on the Main Street revitalization project, and a public hearing on proposed zoning changes in the downtown district.

Agenda Highlights

  • Approval of previous meeting minutes
  • FY2027 budget first reading
  • Main Street project update presentation
  • Public hearing: Downtown zoning amendment
  • Resolution on park improvement bonds
  • Public comment period
values.location: City Hall Council Chambers, 200 Main Street

values.title: Planning Commission Meeting - April 2026
values.body:
The Planning Commission will review several development proposals, including a mixed-use project on Commerce Boulevard and a residential subdivision in the Northfield area. Public input is encouraged on all agenda items.

Items for Review

  • Site plan review: Commerce Boulevard mixed-use development
  • Preliminary plat: Northfield Estates subdivision
  • Variance request: 450 Oak Street setback modification
  • Comprehensive plan amendment discussion
values.location: City Hall Conference Room A, 200 Main Street

values.title: Spring Community Town Hall
values.body:
Join Mayor Chen and city staff for a community town hall meeting to discuss priorities for the upcoming year. Topics include infrastructure improvements, public safety initiatives, and the new city parks master plan. Light refreshments will be provided.

Discussion Topics

  • Infrastructure investment priorities
  • Community policing initiatives
  • Parks master plan overview
  • Open question and answer session

values.title: Main Street Renovation Project Breaks Ground
values.body:
The City of Maplewood has officially broken ground on the $12 million Main Street renovation project. The project will transform the downtown corridor with new sidewalks, bike lanes, improved lighting, and upgraded water infrastructure.

"This project represents our commitment to creating a vibrant, walkable downtown that serves residents and attracts businesses," said Mayor Patricia Chen at the groundbreaking ceremony.

Construction is expected to take 18 months, with phased closures to minimize disruption to local businesses. The project is funded through a combination of federal infrastructure grants and city bonds.

values.title: City Releases FY2027 Proposed Budget
values.body:
City Manager David Rodriguez has presented the proposed FY2027 budget of $142 million to the City Council. The budget prioritizes public safety, infrastructure maintenance, and parks improvements while maintaining the current tax rate.

Key highlights include funding for 12 new police officers, a $5 million road resurfacing program, and $3.2 million for the new Riverside Park renovation. Public comment on the budget will be accepted through April 30.

Budget documents are available for review at City Hall, the public library, and online at the city website.

values.title: Riverside Park Grand Opening Celebration
values.body:
The City of Maplewood invites all residents to the grand opening celebration of the newly renovated Riverside Park on Saturday, May 15. The $3.2 million renovation includes new playgrounds, a splash pad, walking trails, and an outdoor amphitheater.

The celebration will feature live music, food trucks, kids activities, and guided tours of the new facilities. Free parking is available at the community center lot.

values.title: About the City of Maplewood
values.body:
The City of Maplewood was incorporated in 1887 and has grown into a thriving community of over 85,000 residents. Located in the heart of the region, Maplewood offers a unique blend of small-town charm and modern amenities.

Our Mission

To provide exceptional public services that enhance the quality of life for all Maplewood residents, foster economic vitality, and ensure a safe, sustainable community for future generations.

Our Values

  • Transparency - We conduct the public's business openly and honestly.
  • Accountability - We are responsible stewards of public resources.
  • Excellence - We strive for the highest standards in public service.
  • Inclusivity - We serve all members of our diverse community equitably.

City Government

Maplewood operates under a council-manager form of government. The City Council consists of the Mayor and six Council Members elected by district. The City Manager is appointed by the Council to oversee daily operations.

values.title: Contact the City of Maplewood
values.body:
General Information

City Hall is located at 200 Main Street, Maplewood. Our main phone number is (555) 234-5000.

Office Hours

Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Closed on weekends and city holidays.

Emergency Services

For emergencies, dial 911. For non-emergency police or fire, call (555) 234-5003.

Report an Issue

To report potholes, streetlight outages, code violations, or other non-emergency issues, please use our online reporting system or call (555) 234-5100.

Public Records Requests

Submit public records requests to the City Clerk's office at clerk@maplewood.gov or visit City Hall during business hours.
